subject: Computer Networking
title: Interference and Noise
description: A fast breakdown of Interference and Noise for builders.

hook.text: Your Wi-Fi slows down when you microwave popcorn. Both devices fight over the same 2.4 GHz frequency.

buildup.text: Interference happens when multiple devices transmit on the same frequency. Signals collide and corrupt each other.

discovery.text: Spread spectrum techniques spread data across multiple frequencies. If one is noisy, others still get through.

twist.text: In dense apartments, dozens of Wi-Fi networks overlap on the same channels. Congestion, not distance, kills performance.

climax.text: The 5 GHz and 6 GHz bands have more channels. Less crowded means less interference and faster speeds.

punchline.text: Wireless isn't just about signal. It's about avoiding noise.

quiz.question: Why does the 5 GHz band often perform better than 2.4 GHz?
options: It has more channels and less interference from other devices
It has longer range than 2.4 GHz
